edges.description: Loss of one SATB2 copy is the major dosage-sensitive driver of the cognitive and palatal phenotype.
Loss of adjacent genes (GLS, MYO1B, TMEFF2) and ectodermal features are attributed to the broader deletion beyond SATB2.
edges.target: Additional contiguous-gene contribution to the behavioural and ectodermal phenotype

edges.description: Reduced SATB2 dosage disrupts midline craniofacial patterning and palate formation.
Reduced SATB2 dosage impairs specification of upper-layer cortical projection neurons.
Reduced SATB2 dosage impairs osteoblast differentiation and bone mineralization.
edges.target: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

edges.description: Failure of palatal shelf fusion produces cleft palate.
Disrupted palatal morphogenesis produces a high-arched palate when overt clefting is absent.
Disturbed craniofacial patterning produces the characteristic facial dysmorphism.
Impaired SATB2-dependent jaw growth and distal mandibular patterning produces micrognathia.
Disrupted craniofacial/odontogenic development produces dental crowding and abnormally shaped teeth.
Defective cortical neuron specification produces intellectual disability.
Disrupted neurodevelopment presents as global developmental delay.
Cortical/speech-network involvement produces severely impaired speech and language development.
The most severe end of the speech phenotype is absent speech.
Impaired osteoblast function reduces bone mineral density.
edges.source: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

edges.description: Low bone mass and increased bone turnover predispose to fractures.
edges.source: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

edges.description: Mechanically weakened bone produces progressive tibial bowing.
edges.source: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

edges.description: Skeletal fragility and vertebral involvement contribute to scoliosis.
edges.source: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

edges.description: Combined gene loss contributes to autistic features within the behavioural spectrum.
edges.source: Additional contiguous-gene contribution to the behavioural and ectodermal phenotype

edges.description: The recurrent behavioural pattern includes aggression and self-injury.
edges.source: Additional contiguous-gene contribution to the behavioural and ectodermal phenotype

edges.description: Sleeping problems are part of the recurrent behavioural phenotype.
edges.source: Additional contiguous-gene contribution to the behavioural and ectodermal phenotype

edges.description: Thin, sparse hair is an ectodermal feature attributed to broader 2q32-q33 deletion beyond SATB2.
intermediate_mechanisms: loss of additional ectodermal genes in larger deletions
edges.source: Additional contiguous-gene contribution to the behavioural and ectodermal phenotype

edges.description: Elevated osteocalcin reports increased bone turnover/formation arising from the SATB2-dependent osteoblast program.
edges.source: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

edges.description: Elevated P1NP reports increased osteoblastic bone-formation activity.
edges.source: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

edges.description: Elevated alkaline phosphatase reports increased bone turnover.
edges.source: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

edges.description: Elevated urinary N-telopeptide reports increased bone resorption / turnover.
edges.source: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

nodes.description: Characteristic but nonspecific facial dysmorphism aids recognition of the syndrome.

evidence.snippet: In humans, chromosomal translocations and deletions of 2q33.1 leading to SATB2 haploinsufficiency are associated with cleft palate (CP), facial dysmorphism and intellectual disability (ID).

nodes.description: Dental anomalies (crowding, abnormally shaped teeth, hypodontia, delayed eruption) are very common.

evidence.snippet: An anomaly of RS spectrum was found in half the cases, and dental malformations were described in 90%.

nodes.description: Many affected individuals never develop functional speech.

evidence.snippet: Recurrent clinical features included neurodevelopmental impairment (19/19), absent/near absent speech (16/19), normal somatic growth (17/19), cleft palate (9/19), drooling (12/19), and dental anomalies (8/19).

nodes.description: Beyond SATB2, loss of neighbouring genes is thought to shape the more complex behavioural phenotype (with the glutaminase gene GLS and MYO1B/TMEFF2 proposed as candidates), while thin skin and sparse hair in patients with larger deletions are attributed to loss of additional, as-yet-undefined ectodermal genes.
nodes.id: Additional contiguous-gene contribution to the behavioural and ectodermal phenotype

evidence.snippet: it is our opinion that the SATB2 gene is also crucial to the behavioural problems noted in this current proband and that deletion of the GLS, MYO1B, and TMEFF2 genes presumably contributes to the more complex behavioural characteristics observed
Only one of the subjects presented here had a cleft palate, suggesting reduced penetrance for this feature.

nodes.description: A specific behavioural pattern with aggression, self-injury, and motor restlessness has been described.

evidence.snippet: A specific behavioural pattern, with hyperactivity and motor restlessness, chaotic behaviour, a happy personality but with periods of aggression and anxiety, sleeping problems and self-mutilation, has also been reported in some of these patients.
Elevations in alkaline phosphatase, urinary N-telopeptide/creatinine ratio, and osteocalcin in the patient indicate increased bone turnover.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated alkaline phosphatase reports increased bone turnover.
readouts.target: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

nodes.description: Autistic features are part of the recurrent behavioural phenotype, alongside a frequently jovial/friendly personality.

evidence.snippet: The behavioral phenotype appeared to show the most marked discrepancy within the cohort, with both severe autistic features and friendly/happy personalities being recurrently reported.

nodes.description: Cleft palate is a characteristic craniofacial feature, although penetrance is incomplete and a high-arched palate may occur instead.

evidence.snippet: Recurrent clinical features included neurodevelopmental impairment (19/19), absent/near absent speech (16/19), normal somatic growth (17/19), cleft palate (9/19), drooling (12/19), and dental anomalies (8/19).

nodes.description: Interstitial deletion of chromosome 2q32-q33 removes one copy of a contiguous block of genes. The commonly deleted region contains SATB2 plus neighbouring dosage-sensitive genes (GLS, MYO1B, TMEFF2, PGAP1, among others), so the phenotype reflects combined haploinsufficiency, with SATB2 loss as the major driver of the cognitive and craniofacial/palatal features.

evidence.snippet: The commonly deleted region contains at least seven genes.
The deletion encompassed 22 known OMIM genes, including GLS, MYO1B, TMEFF2, PGAP1 and SATB2.

nodes.description: SATB2 specifies upper-layer cortico-cortical projection neurons and the formation of callosal axonal projections. Its loss misroutes these neurons, providing a mechanism for the severe intellectual disability and the profound speech/language impairment.

evidence.snippet: In mice, studies have found a critical role for Satb2 in brain development, particularly in the specification of cortical upper layer neurons

Vertebrate animal models have shown that Satb2 has a crucial role in craniofacial patterning and osteoblast differentiation, as well as in determining the fates of neuronal projections in the developing neocortex.

nodes.description: SATB2 controls midline craniofacial patterning, jaw growth, and palate formation. Its loss disrupts roof-of-mouth development, producing cleft or high-arched palate, micrognathia, dental anomalies, and facial dysmorphism.

evidence.snippet: In humans, chromosomal translocations and deletions of 2q33.1 leading to SATB2 haploinsufficiency are associated with cleft palate (CP), facial dysmorphism and intellectual disability (ID).
At birth, mice lacking Satb2 have severely reduced jaw length and die from cleft palate

nodes.description: Severe speech and language impairment is one of the most consistent and distinctive features.

evidence.snippet: A major language delay was described in all patients; 65% had a vocabulary of 10 words or less.

nodes.description: Global developmental delay is a universal early manifestation.

evidence.snippet: SATB2-associated syndrome (SAS) is a multisystem disorder in which all affected individuals have developmental delay / intellectual disability that can range from mild to profound but is most commonly moderate to profound.

nodes.description: A high-arched palate is part of the palatal anomaly spectrum and may occur in the absence of overt clefting.

evidence.snippet: Clinical features of this syndrome include severe mental retardation, growth retardation, dysmorphic features, thin and sparse hair, feeding difficulties and cleft or high palate.

nodes.description: Fractures, including vertebral compression fractures, occur as a consequence of bone fragility.

evidence.snippet: Vertebral compression fractures affected 6/17 patients (35%).

nodes.description: Developmental delay / intellectual disability is present in essentially all affected individuals and is frequently in the moderate-to-severe range.

evidence.snippet: SATB2-associated syndrome (SAS) is a multisystem disorder in which all affected individuals have developmental delay / intellectual disability that can range from mild to profound but is most commonly moderate to profound.

nodes.description: Micrognathia (small/underdeveloped mandible) is a recurrent craniofacial feature reflecting the SATB2-dependent program for jaw growth and patterning.

evidence.snippet: Behavioral abnormalities, facial dysmorphism, cleft palate, micrognathia, dental anomalies, and musculoskeletal changes have also been reported consistently in all etiological subgroups
The SAS phenotype also includes craniofacial anomalies that include micrognathia and cleft palate. Similarly, Satb2 knock‐out mice display hypoplasia of the distal jaw skeleton.

nodes.description: SATB2 promotes osteoblast differentiation and bone mineralization (in part by regulating osteogenic transcription). Its haploinsufficiency reduces bone mineral density and, particularly with alleles that escape nonsense-mediated decay, produces increased bone turnover, fractures, tibial bowing, and scoliosis.
nodes.id: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

evidence.snippet: We conclude that SATB2 pathogenic variants are responsible for skeletal demineralization and osteoporosis. We found increased levels of bone formation markers, supporting the key role of SATB2 in osteoblast differentiation.
loss of Satb2 leads to reduced levels of bone mineralization, resulting in short and brittle limb bones
osteocalcin and serum procollagen type 1 amino-terminal propeptide (P1NP) were both increased. Vitamin D insufficiency was frequent (66.7%).
Elevations in alkaline phosphatase, urinary N-telopeptide/creatinine ratio, and osteocalcin in the patient indicate increased bone turnover.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated osteocalcin reports increased bone turnover/formation arising from the SATB2-dependent osteoblast program.
readouts.target: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

evidence.snippet: osteocalcin and serum procollagen type 1 amino-terminal propeptide (P1NP) were both increased. Vitamin D insufficiency was frequent (66.7%).

readouts.interpretation: Elevated P1NP reports increased osteoblastic bone-formation activity.
readouts.target: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility

nodes.description: Reduced bone mineral density / osteoporosis is part of the skeletal phenotype and warrants surveillance. Reported frequency is strongly ascertainment-dependent: a dedicated skeletal-imaging cohort found skeletal demineralization in 94% (Mouillé et al., PMID:35241104), whereas GeneReviews estimates documented low bone mineral density in only about one quarter of affected individuals (PMID:29023086), reflecting the difference between systematic skeletal phenotyping and routine clinical ascertainment.

evidence.snippet: Skeletal demineralization (16/17, 94%) and cortical thinning of vertebrae (15/17) were the most frequent radiological features at the spine.

Haploinsufficiency of one of these genes, SATB2, a DNA-binding protein that regulates gene expression, has been implicated as causative in the cleft or high palate of individuals with 2q32q33 microdeletion syndrome.
SATB2 haploinsufficiency is a common cause of syndromic intellectual disability.

nodes.description: Scoliosis is part of the skeletal phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Skeletal anomalies can include scoliosis, tibial bowing, and joint contractures.

nodes.description: Sleeping problems recur within the behavioural phenotype.

evidence.snippet: A specific behavioural pattern, with hyperactivity and motor restlessness, chaotic behaviour, a happy personality but with periods of aggression and anxiety, sleeping problems and self-mutilation, has also been reported in some of these patients.

nodes.description: Thin and sparse hair, often with thin skin and reduced subcutaneous fat, is an ectodermal feature seen particularly with larger deletions.

evidence.snippet: Individuals with deletions of 2q32q33 and who present with learning difficulties, growth retardation, dysmorphic features, thin and sparse hair, feeding difficulties, and high or cleft palate have been classified as having a new microdeletion syndrome.

nodes.description: Progressive tibial bowing is reported, particularly with SATB2 alleles associated with more severe bone disease.

evidence.snippet: We report a SATB2 mutation (c.2018dupA; p.(H673fs)) in a 15-year-old patient whose SATB2-associated syndrome phenotype is accompanied by osteoporosis, fractures, progressive tibial bowing, and scoliosis.
Elevations in alkaline phosphatase, urinary N-telopeptide/creatinine ratio, and osteocalcin in the patient indicate increased bone turnover.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated urinary N-telopeptide reports increased bone resorption / turnover.
readouts.target: Osteoblast differentiation defect and bone fragility
